label: Slavery, Reconstruction & Civil Rights

sources.title: History 393 – Slavery, Civil War, and Reconstruction

app_prompts: Reconstruction amendments explainer: user explores the 13th, 14th, and 15th amendments — what each said, what freedpeople expected, what actually happened, and how Reconstruction was dismantled.

Great Migration tracker: user picks a Southern city of origin and traces the migration routes north and west — what pushed people out, what drew them to Chicago or Detroit, what they found when they arrived.
Civil rights movement timeline: user picks a year and sees the key events, the organizations involved, the government responses, and the local and national tensions that shaped the movement.

Racial capitalism case study: user picks an era (slavery, Reconstruction, Jim Crow, mass incarceration) and sees how racial hierarchy was encoded into economic structures — labor control, property law, credit.

Black feminist thought reader: user selects a concept from Crenshaw, Collins, or hooks — intersectionality, the matrix of domination, the politics of respectability — and explores what it means with historical examples.
